description.content: Import large volumes of users and events asynchronously using batch import jobs that support up to 50,000 records per request.

description.content: Imports up to 50,000 users in a single asynchronous request. Returns a job object with an ID and status that can be polled for completion. Users can be anonymous or identified with a uid.

Send custom server-side events to FullStory. Events can be associated with sessions and users, and include custom properties for behavioral analysis.

Creates one custom event with the specified details. Events can be associated with a session and user. If use_most_recent is true, the most recent session within 30 minutes will be used. Custom properties can be attached and types are inferred automatically in v2.

Create, retrieve, update, and delete users in FullStory. Users can be anonymous or identified with a uid. Custom properties can be attached to enrich user profiles.

Creates a new user or updates an existing user if a matching uid is found. Anonymous users can be created by omitting the uid field. The API returns the FullStory-assigned id along with the user data. Type suffixes are not required for custom properties in v2 as types are inferred automatically.

The FullStory Server API is a RESTful API that enables developers to programmatically send user and event data to FullStory. It supports creating and updating individual users, batch importing users, sending server-side events, and batch importing events. The API uses JSON for request and response bodies and authenticates via API keys. Developers can use it to enrich FullStory session data with server-side context that is not available in the browser or mobile app.
